page.title: osteoarthritis — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for osteoarthritis: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for osteoarthritis: 128 alterations and 302 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Therapeutic potential of senolytic agent quercetin in osteoarthritis.
Boswellia and its extract may be an effective and safe treatment option for patient with osteoarthritis.
Clinicians may consider Tai Chi as a viable complementary and alternative medicine for chronic pain conditions.
Combined exercise interventions appear to be effective in preserving postmenopausal women's BMD at lumbar spine, femoral neck, hip and total body.
Curative effect of electroacupuncture and manual acupuncture for knee osteoarthritis.
Effectiveness of Tai Chi for health promotion for adults with health conditions: a scoping review of Meta-analyses.
Electroacupuncture treatment can relieve the pain of osteoarthritis of the knees and improve comprehensive aspects of knee osteoarthritis.
Tai Chi was beneficial for chronic conditions-cancer, osteoarthritis, heart failure and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
The efficacy and safety of Curcuma longa Extract and curcumin supplements on osteoarthritis.
The present review provides the evidence of LLLT-dependent reduction of IL-1β, TNF-α, and MMP-13.
These RCTs provide scientific evidence that supports the efficacy of turmeric extract and curcumin in the treatment of arthritis.
A Boswellia serrata extract safely reduces pain and improves physical functioning significantly in osteoarthritis patients.
A combination of baicalin (a compound found within Chinese skullcap) and catechin (a polyphenol plant antioxidant) are as effective as naproxen in treating osteoarthritis of the knee.
A combination of fish oil, nettle and vitamin E (Phytalgic) decreases the neeed for analgesics and NSAIDs and improves symptoms of osteoarthritis.
A combination of glucosamine, chondroitin and quercetin significantly improve the symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.
A combination of mud-bath therapy and bicarbonate-sulphate mineral bath water result in a slight decrease in serum leptin levels and an increase in adipnectin levels in patients with osteoarthritis.
A combination of Phellodendron and Citrus peel extract has a beneficial effect on cardiovascular risk factors; namely lipid levels, blood pressure and fasting glucose levels.
A complex of curcumin and phosphatidylcholine is effective in reducing the symptoms of ostearthritis.
A curcumin extract is appropriate for the care of patients with joint problems and may also contribute to the regeneration of cartilage.
A homeopathic complex with physiotherapy could significantly improve symptoms associated with chronic low-back pain due to osteoarthritis.
A homeopathic gel compares favorably to NSAID gel for treating osteoarthritis of the knee.
A moderate T'ai Chi intervention can enhance arthritis self-efficacy, quality of life, and functional mobility among older adults with osteoarthritis.
A new type of very low-power modulated laser: soft-tissue changes induced in osteoarthritic patients revealed by sonography.
A novel nonsurgical therapy for peri-implantitis using focused pulsed electromagnetic field.
A seaweed derived mineral supplement may increase range of motion and walking distances in subjects with osteoarthritis of the knee and has a drug sparing effect.
A single-session multi-focal low-level laser therapy on quadriceps in knee osteoarthritis patients has immediate beneficial effect on knee pain reduction.

A water soluble extract of Panax notoginseng, Rehmannia glutinosa and Eleutherococcus senticosus is safe and effective for symptomatic improvement of pain and physical function in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
A Yoga based regimen is effective in providing relief in osteoarthritis of the hands.
Acupuncture improves symptoms of chronic shoulder pain diagnosed as osteoarthritis or rotator cuff tendonitis.
Acupuncture therapy offered in the group setting was effective in reducing pain severity.
Air pollution and risk of 32 health conditions: outcome-wide analyses in a population-based prospective cohort.
Ambient green light reduces knee pain in osteoarthritis patients.
An extract from the seed of the African shea tree slows the disease process in patients with the highest levels of osteoarthritis biomarkers.
Applications of photobiomodulation therapy to musculoskeletal disorders and osteoarthritis with particular relevance to Canada.
Association between glyphosate exposure and osteoarthritis in US adults.
Avocado/soybean unsaponifiables are safe and effective in the treatment of symptomatic osteoarthritis of the knee and hip.
Balneotherapy and mud-pack therapy were effective in treating patients with knee osteoarthritis.
Balneotherapy has positive effects on gait properties and clinical health quality parameters of patients with knee osteoarthritis in short-term evaluations.
Beeswax contains compounds which protect against NSAID-induced gastric ulcers in patients being treated for ostearthritis.
Benefits of a food supplement containing Boswellia serrata and bromelain for improving the quality of life in patients with osteoarthritis.
